%════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
% Chameleon Mechanism — Papers I, VII, V, XIII
%════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
% rem:chameleon — in Paper I remarks (unlabeled in source)

\begin{proposition}[Condensate chameleon screening at the QCD scale]
\label{P5:prop:qcd_chameleon}
Inside a nucleon, the condensate density is driven to equilibrium
with the QCD energy density.
At the confinement scale $\LQCD\approx208\,\mathrm{MeV}$:
\begin{equation}
  \bst\rho_{\mathrm{nucleon}}
  \;\sim\; \frac{\LQCD^4}{\Lcond^4}
  \;=\; \left(\frac{\LQCD}{\Lcond}\right)^{\!4}
  \;\approx\; \left(\frac{208\,\mathrm{MeV}}{1.19\times10^{-4}\,\mathrm{eV}}\right)^{\!4}
  \;\approx\; 9.3\times10^{48}.
  \label{P13:eq:rho_nucleon}
\end{equation}
The condensate correction to quark kinetic energy inside the nucleon is
\begin{equation}
  \varepsilon_{\mathrm{nucleon}}
  \;=\; \frac{1}{\vp^6(1+\bst\rho_{\mathrm{nucleon}})}
  \;\approx\; 6.0\times10^{-51},
  \label{P13:eq:eps_nucleon}
\end{equation}
completely negligible relative to the QCD coupling $\alpha_s(\LQCD)\approx1$.
\end{proposition}
\status{published}
\source{P5:prop:qcd_chameleon}

\begin{theorem}[Chameleon screening: spherical harmonics from first principles]
\label{P13:thm:chameleon_screening}
In the chameleon limit $\bst\rhoa\gg1$, the condensate correction
$\Hcond = -(\hbar^2/2m_e)(\epsr/r^2)L^2$ vanishes uniformly
in all directions:
\begin{equation}
  \epsr \;\to\; 0
  \qquad \text{as } \bst\rhoa \to \infty.
  \label{P13:eq:chameleon_limit}
\end{equation}
The effective Hamiltonian reduces to the standard isotropic
Coulomb Hamiltonian:
\begin{equation}
  \Heff \;\to\; -\frac{\hbar^2}{2m_e}\nabla^2 - \frac{e^2}{r}
  \qquad (\bst\rhoa \gg 1).
  \label{P13:eq:H_standard}
\end{equation}
The eigenfunctions of~\eqref{P13:eq:H_standard} are
$\psi_{n\ell m}(r,\theta,\varphi) = R_{n\ell}(r)\,Y_\ell^m(\theta,\varphi)$,
where $Y_\ell^m$ are the standard spherical harmonics.
Spherical harmonics are therefore the correct orbital shapes
within the condensate framework, to all measurable precision.
\end{theorem}
\status{published}
\source{P13:thm:chameleon_screening}


%════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
% Chameleon Equations — Three-Scale Hierarchy
%════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

% Chameleon screening hierarchy (from Papers VII, XIII, V):
%
%  Environment      beta*rho          epsilon           Effect
%  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
%  Cosmic attractor  phi = 1.618 (exact)  1/phi^8 ≈ 0.021  Active: sets all SM parameters
%  Bohr radius       ~2.6e25              ~2.2e-27          Screened: orbital shapes = Y_l^m
%  QCD scale         ~9.3e48              ~6e-51            Negligible: standard QCD free
%
% Key identity: beta*rho_inf = phi (exact), 1+phi = phi^2 (pentagon), epsilon_inf = 1/phi^8

\begin{equation}
  \epsr \;\to\; 0
  \qquad \text{as } \bst\rhoa \to \infty.
\end{equation}

\begin{equation}
  \Delta E_{n\ell}
  \;\approx\; -\frac{\ell(\ell+1)\,\Lcond^4}{\vp^6\,\aem^4\,m_e^3}
              \cdot\frac{\langle r^2\rangle_{n\ell}}{a_0^4},
  \label{P13:eq:delta_E_chameleon}
\end{equation}

\begin{equation}
  \eps(a_0)
  \;=\; \frac{1}{\vp^6(1+\bst\rhoa(a_0))}
  \;\approx\; 2.2\times10^{-27}.
  \label{P13:eq:eps_a0_value}
\end{equation}

\begin{equation}
  \eps(\rhoinf)
  \;=\; \frac{1}{\vp^6(1+\bst\rhoinf)}
  \;=\; \frac{1}{\vp^6(1+\vp)}
  \;=\; \frac{1}{\vp^6\cdot\vp^2}
  \;=\; \frac{1}{\vp^8},
  \label{P13:eq:eps_attractor}
\end{equation}

\begin{equation}
  \bst\rho_{\rm atom}(a_0)
  \;\approx\; \frac{\aem^6 m_e^4}{2\Lcond^4}
  \;\approx\; 2.57\times10^{25},
  \label{P13:eq:rho_atom_a0}
\end{equation}

\begin{equation}
  \varepsilon_{\mathrm{nucleon}}
  \;=\; \frac{1}{\vp^6(1+\bst\rho_{\mathrm{nucleon}})}
  \;\approx\; 6.0\times10^{-51},
\end{equation}

\begin{equation}
  \bst\rho_{\mathrm{nucleon}}
  \;\sim\; \frac{\LQCD^4}{\Lcond^4}
  \;=\; \left(\frac{\LQCD}{\Lcond}\right)^{\!4}
  \;\approx\; \left(\frac{208\,\mathrm{MeV}}{1.19\times10^{-4}\,\mathrm{eV}}\right)^{\!4}
  \;\approx\; 9.3\times10^{48}.
\end{equation}
